sense.definition: a competition in which the best player or team is chosen

examples.x: Their victory in the championship was celebrated by fans nationwide.
The final round of the golf championship will take place on Sunday.
Fans are eagerly awaiting the championship game to see who will take home the trophy.
The city will host the basketball championship for the first time in its history.
She trained rigorously in preparation for the upcoming tennis championship.

sense.definition: a series of matches played by a particular sports teams in various locations

examples.x: During the basketball team's national tour, they faced various opponents in different cities, building team chemistry and fan engagement.
As part of the baseball league schedule, the team went on a West Coast tour, competing against rival teams in multiple states.
The tennis player participated in an exhibition tour, showcasing skills and engaging with fans in various tennis clubs across the country.
To promote the sport of rugby, the national team organized a tour, playing matches in schools and communities to encourage participation.
The soccer team embarked on a preseason tour, playing friendly matches in different countries to prepare for the upcoming season.

sense.definition: an initial round or stage that comes before the main competition, often used to determine who qualifies or how participants are placed

examples.x: They competed in the prelim round to qualify for the finals.

sense.definition: a round or series of rounds where competitors who have been previously defeated have another opportunity to continue in the competition

examples.x: He lost in the early rounds but had another chance to qualify through the repechage.
The repechage round allowed her to advance to the semi-finals despite losing in the quarter-finals.
They competed in the repechage to earn a spot in the finals.
After losing in the preliminaries, he trained hard to succeed in the repechage.
The judo tournament included a repechage round for those who didn't advance directly.

sense.definition: the last match, race, etc., in a competition that determines the champion

examples.x: Reaching the final was an incredible achievement for the underdog team.
The final of the tennis tournament drew a large crowd of excited fans.

sense.definition: one of the two competitions before the final round

examples.x: After winning the semifinal, she was excited but nervous about the upcoming final.
He celebrated his victory in the semifinal, knowing that the hardest match was still to come.
The tournament's semifinal is scheduled for tomorrow, and the players are preparing for a tough game.

sense.definition: a competition where teams are composed of one male and one female player

examples.x: Mixed doubles require good coordination between partners.
She prefers mixed doubles over singles matches.
They advanced to the finals in mixed doubles badminton.
He and his partner practiced hard for the mixed doubles tournament.
Mixed doubles pairings can be challenging but fun.

sense.definition: a group of sports clubs or players who compete against each other and are put together based on the points they have gained through the season

examples.x: He joined a local league to play basketball every weekend.
The team won the championship in their league last year.
She played for a league that only allows amateur players.
Professional athletes often compete in international leagues.

sense.definition: a competition in which two players or teams compete against one another such as soccer, boxing, etc.

examples.x: The soccer match ended in a tie, with both teams scoring two goals each.
After a long and intense match, the tennis player emerged victorious and celebrated with her fans.
The local cricket club organized a friendly match to raise funds for charity.
He trained hard for the upcoming match, determined to improve his performance and win.

sense.definition: a sports event with two players or teams competing against one another

examples.x: The matchup between the two top teams is expected to be thrilling.
Fans are excited for the upcoming matchup between the rivals.
The coaches are analyzing the strengths and weaknesses of the upcoming matchup.
The tournament features some interesting matchups that will keep everyone on their toes.
I’m looking forward to watching the matchup between the defending champions and the underdogs.

sense.definition: a non-competitive match or game played for entertainment, practice, or promotional purposes, rather than as part of a formal competition or league

examples.x: The basketball team played an exhibition game to showcase their skills before the season started.
Fans enjoyed watching their favorite players in the exhibition game against a local college team.
The soccer club organized an exhibition game to raise funds for charity.
The baseball team scheduled an exhibition game to warm up before the regular season.
She scored a hat trick in the exhibition game against the visiting team.

sense.definition: the top professional football league in England, comprising 20 clubs competing for the championship title

examples.x: Premier League clubs attract top talent from around the globe.
The Premier League is widely regarded as one of the most competitive football leagues in the world.
Manchester United has won the Premier League title multiple times.
Broadcast rights for Premier League matches are highly lucrative.
Premier League matches are played in stadiums across England, with some also held abroad.

sense.definition: a competitive international match in sports such as cricket or rugby, serving as a formal test of skill between national teams

examples.x: He scored a century in the test match against the visiting team.
The test match ended in a draw after five days of intense play.
They won the final test match of the season to clinch the series.
The rugby players faced a tough challenge in the test match against the reigning champions.
Fans traveled from across the country to watch the test match at the historic stadium.

sense.definition: a professional American football league comprising 32 teams
a series of sporting games in which teams or players compete against different rivals in different rounds until only one remains and that is the winner

examples.x: The team trained hard for months in preparation for the regional soccer tournament.
She won the chess tournament after defeating numerous challengers in several rounds.
The annual tennis tournament attracts top players from around the world.
They advanced to the final round of the basketball tournament, facing their toughest rivals yet.
The local golf tournament raised funds for charity while showcasing impressive talent.

sense.definition: a substance used by athletes to improve athletic performance, often banned by sports organizations due to unfair advantages

examples.x: There is a zero-tolerance policy for performance-enhancing drugs in professional sports.
The use of performance-enhancing drugs can have serious health consequences.
Using performance-enhancing drugs violates the rules of fair play in sports.
The athlete was caught using performance-enhancing drugs and faced a suspension.
Athletes who use performance-enhancing drugs risk disqualification from competition.

sense.definition: a set of rules or conditions that are put in place to balance the game and give a disadvantaged player a better chance of winning
a special additional round or rule used to determine a winner when the score is equal after regular play

examples.x: They played a tiebreaker to decide the winner of the tennis match.
He won the tiebreaker round by scoring the first point.
The tiebreaker rules were explained to the teams before the playoff game.
The volleyball match ended with an exciting tiebreaker set.
The chess match reached a stalemate and was decided by a tiebreaker.

sense.definition: an event or contest in which individuals or teams compete against each other

examples.x: The annual chess competition draws players from around the world.
The basketball competition was exciting, and our team won.
The city will host a soccer competition next month.
The competition for the best home-made cake is tomorrow.
The dance competition at the festival was the highlight of the night.

sense.definition: the traditional Scottish athletic competitions that feature events like hammer throwing

examples.x: They attended the highland games to watch the athletes compete.
The highland games showcase Scotland's rich cultural heritage.
Participants wear traditional Scottish attire at the highland games.
Heavyweight athletes compete in stone putting at the highland games.
The highland games offer a unique experience for visitors to Scotland.

sense.definition: the athletic events where participants showcase strength through various feats of power, such as lifting heavy objects and pulling vehicles

examples.x: He trains extensively for strongman competitions.
Strongman competitions require both physical strength and mental toughness.
Participants prepare for months leading up to strongman competitions.
She aspires to compete in international strongman competitions.
Strongman competitions attract athletes from around the world.

sense.definition: to play a match again, due to specific circumstances such as a tie or inconclusive outcome

examples.x: In the basketball tournament, a technical issue occurred, and officials decided to replay the interrupted match.
The soccer match ended in a tie, leading to a decision to replay the game to determine the winner.
In the event of a draw, chess players may opt to replay the match to establish a clear winner.

sense.definition: the period in a game when the outcome is virtually decided, and less experienced or non-starting players are often given playing time

examples.x: She managed to make a three-pointer during garbage time.
The intensity of the game dropped significantly during garbage time.
They maintained a comfortable lead throughout garbage time.
The announcers discussed the team's performance during garbage time.
The coach used garbage time to experiment with different lineup combinations.

sense.definition: a race format where individuals or teams compete against the clock to achieve the fastest time

examples.x: The cyclist set a new record in the time trial by completing the course in under 30 minutes.
She finished first in the time trial, earning the top spot in the competition.
The coach emphasized pacing during the time trial so that no one would tire out too quickly.
He struggled during the time trial but still managed to finish in the top ten.

sense.definition: the initial round where teams compete within groups to advance

examples.x: The group stage is crucial for securing a good position in the tournament.
Fans eagerly watched the final group stage match to see who would advance.
A surprise upset occurred during the group stage of the tournament.
They were eliminated in the group stage despite their strong performance.
Each team plays multiple opponents in the group stage.

sense.definition: the highest level of competition within a particular league or division

examples.x: Winning the Premiership is the ultimate goal for many athletes and teams.

sense.definition: a round or stage of a tournament, where the number of participants is reduced to eight

examples.x: They advanced to the quarterfinals after winning their preliminary matches.
The tennis player faced a tough opponent in the quarterfinal of the tournament.
He scored the winning goal in overtime to secure a spot in the quarterfinals.
The quarterfinals of the chess tournament featured intense matches.
Fans packed the stadium to watch the quarterfinals of the soccer championship.

sense.definition: the international competitions where athletes or teams from various countries compete to determine the best in their sport

examples.x: He won gold at the world championships.
The team is training for the world championship.
They hosted the world championships in their city.
He broke a record at the world championship.
They are the current world championships winners.

sense.definition: a set of championships or matches happening in a particular sport including tennis, golf, or rugby that are of great significance

examples.x: She achieved a rare feat by securing a grand slam victory in the swimming world championships, winning gold medals in all of her individual events.
The boxer aspired to win a grand slam of world titles by becoming the champion in multiple weight divisions, cementing his legacy as one of the greatest fighters of all time.

sense.definition: a sports match or event where both teams are near each other in the league standings, and the result significantly affects their rankings

examples.x: This weekend's match is a crucial six pointer for both teams fighting to avoid relegation.
Every match in the final stretch of the season feels like a six pointer as teams vie for playoff spots.
The derby match turned out to be a six pointer as both teams battled for the top spot in the league.
With only a few games left, every encounter becomes a potential six pointer for teams chasing promotion.
Tonight's fixture is a six pointer; a win could lift us out of the relegation zone.

sense.definition: to participate in a race

examples.x: The sled dogs ran the Iditarod race with incredible determination.
I used to run cross-country in high school.
She ran her first 10K race and finished with a smile.

sense.definition: a race or competition that spans a distance of one hundred units, such as meters, kilometers, or miles

examples.x: The local track club organized a century through the city streets.
The athletes prepared for the century, a grueling 100-meter dash.
He set a new personal record in the century, completing 100 kilometers in under six hours.
Completing a century swim is a notable achievement in open water competitions.
Every year, the town hosts a century walk to celebrate its founding.

sense.definition: of a game or match, where a large number of points or goals are scored

examples.x: The basketball team won the high-scoring game with a final score of 120-115.
In a surprising turn, the soccer match ended in a high-scoring draw of 4-4.
Despite the high-scoring first half, the second half of the baseball game was a defensive battle.
The hockey game was a high-scoring affair, with both teams netting over six goals each.
The tennis match turned into a high-scoring marathon, lasting over five hours.

sense.definition: a competitive contest where athletes perform dives from a springboard or platform into water, aiming for skill, technique, and grace

examples.x: She won a gold medal in the diving event at the national championships.
The diving event at the Olympics showcased incredible athleticism and precision.
Athletes train rigorously for the diving event, focusing on perfecting their dives.
Judges scored each dive in the diving event based on form and execution.
He practiced his twists and flips for weeks leading up to the diving event.
